name: Drunken Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 cups pinto beans, soaked and drained
freshly ground black pepper -- to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, cook the bacon over medium heat until rendered.
Remove the bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside.
2. Add 1 tablespoon of reserved fat to the saucepan. Cook the onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes until translucent.
3. Add pinto beans, chicken stock or water, and beer. Bring to a boil.
4. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook for about 1 hour, checking every 30 minutes.
5. After 30 minutes, add jalapeno chiles. Adjust liquid as needed to keep beans covered.
6. When beans are tender, season with salt and pepper.
7. Stir in cilantro and serve.
Notes: Chef Stephan Pyles included this recipe in his cookbook "The New Texas Cuisine," Doubleday, 1993. He suggests serving Drunken Beans with Jalapeno-Stuffed Smoked Dove, Three Potato Salad, and Rosy Relish, all also found in his cookbook.
